rationale: The citing paper uses Ding, Grewal, and Liechty (2005) as a methodological basis for its incentive-compatible conjoint design, explicitly pointing readers to that work when introducing its incentive-aligned elicitation approach. This is not just a background literature list; the cited work supports a core design choice used to measure willingness to pay with consequential choices. The citing paper does not refine or challenge Ding et al., but uses the method logic in a new empirical context (misinformation and debunking).
relation_description: The citing paper applies the incentive-aligned conjoint approach associated with Ding et al. (2005) to design consequential choice tasks for estimating WTP under misinformation and debunking treatments. It uses the cited article as methodological precedent rather than as a target of extension or critique.
